qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded decompression in CompressedData packet processing enables denial of service via crafted OpenPGP messages
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a panic in the rsa crate during RSA secret key packet parsing (CVE-2026-21895), while this finding describes unbounded memory allocation during CompressedData packet decompression. Different packet types (CompressedData vs RSA secret key), different root causes (missing decompression size limit vs unhandled edge case in RSA key construction), and different attack vectors (zip-bomb amplification vs crafted RSA parameters).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory describes CVE-2026-21895, a panic in the Rust Crypto rsa crate triggered through rPGP's RSA secret key packet parsing. Neither qualifying finding addresses RSA secret key packet handling or the rsa crate edge case. Finding 'unbounded-decompression-dos' describes a different DoS vector (unbounded decompression in CompressedData packets) with a different root cause. Finding 'example' describes an unrelated Node.js deserialization RCE in a different codebase.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-7587-4wv6-m68m describes CVE-2026-21895: panic during RSA secret key packet parsing via unhandled edge case in rsa crate
Affected component: RSA secret key packet parsing in rPGP, specifically the rsa crate key construction path
Finding 'unbounded-decompression-dos': CompressedData packet decompression, no RSA key handling, different root cause
Finding 'example': Node.js import endpoint deserialization, completely different ecosystem and codebase
